news.title: Kalki Koechlin sells apartment in Mumbai for ₹2.55 crore
news.para: Actress Kalki Koechlin has sold her Mumbai apartment in Varsova Kiran Co-operative Housing Society for ₹2.55 crore. Purchased in 2015 for ₹1.95 crore, the sale reflects an appreciation of approximately ₹60 lakh over a decade. The transaction, registered in April 2026, saw a stamp duty payment of ₹16.08 lakh.

news.title: From Krishna Curve to Krishna CONIQ: Redefining the future of urban retail destinations
news.para: Krishna United Group is transforming Mumbai's Linking Road into a lifestyle hub. Following the success of Krishna Curve, the company is launching Krishna CONIQ. This ambitious project integrates retail, dining, entertainment, and office spaces. It aims to create an immersive experience engaging all five senses. Krishna CONIQ represents the future of urban retail and lifestyle destinations in India.

news.title: Delhi: Crackdown on illegal constructions ramps up; 217 properties razed, 237 sealed in June so far
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi has intensified its crackdown on illegal constructions. On Monday, 14 properties were demolished and 25 establishments sealed. This drive follows a hotel fire incident. Since then, 217 properties have been demolished and 237 sealed.

news.title: India’s REIT, InvIT AUM may double to ₹20 lakh crore by 2030: Report
news.para: Avendus Capital estimates that India’s REIT and InvIT market could attract additional investments of ₹11.6 lakh crore over the next five years. Domestic mutual funds, insurance companies and pension funds are expected to be among the key sources of capital.

news.title: China's new home prices fall at faster pace in May on soft demand
news.para: China's new home prices declined at a faster pace in May, signaling continued struggles in the property sector despite tentative stabilization in larger cities. While major urban centers saw modest price increases, smaller cities experienced extended declines, highlighting a divergent market. Property sales and investment also fell sharply in the first five months of the year.

news.title: Race Course slums: Can't demolish houses of residents yet to relocate, says Delhi HC
news.para: The Delhi High Court has ordered authorities not to demolish houses of residents in Race Course slum clusters who have not accepted alternative accommodation until July 1. This comes after residents appealed an eviction order, citing ongoing litigation and a demolition drive on Sunday. The court emphasized that rehabilitation is for those willing to move voluntarily.

news.title: Dharavi redevelopment project uses ‘digital twin’ for resident verification
news.para: Dharavi is getting a digital twin as part of a 10-year redevelopment project. This digital replica captures detailed information about every structure and resident, aiding in the verification of claims for rehabilitation housing. Advanced technologies like drones and LiDAR have been employed to map the entire slum, ensuring accurate data for the project's execution.

news.title: Lucknow development body to launch Varun Vihar & Naimish Nagar housing schemes
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is launching its Varun Vihar and Naimish Nagar housing schemes, offering over 3,100 residential plots in the first phase. Online registration opens for plots ranging from 72 to 450 sq m, with Varun Vihar planned for 6,580 acres and Naimish Nagar for 3,600 acres, both featuring modern infrastructure and green spaces.

news.title: Air India building’s repairs to cost ₹181 crore, work to take six months
news.para: The Maharashtra government is set to spend approximately Rs 181 crore on repairing and renovating the recently acquired 22-storey Air India building at Nariman Point. This extensive renovation, expected to take six months, will house state government offices, saving an estimated Rs 200 crore in annual rent. The iconic Air India logo will be replaced with a Maharashtra government emblem.

news.title: About 127 families in Thane still waiting for flats, admits MHADA
news.para: MHADA has acknowledged that 127 families in Vartak Nagar, Thane, are still awaiting homes after redevelopment. This comes as MHADA gave conditional consent for occupation certificates for new buildings. The families are in a long-running dispute over rehabilitation and reduced carpet area. The Bombay High Court is hearing the case.

news.title: CMDA empowered to approve high-rise buildings in Chennai
news.para: The Tamil Nadu government has delegated the authority to approve high-rise building projects in the Chennai Metropolitan Area back to the Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority (CMDA). This move aims to expedite the planning permission process, which previously involved lengthy government approvals. A reconstituted scrutiny panel, chaired by the CMDA member secretary, will now handle these applications.

news.title: Ahmedabad homes to cost 5-10% more from July 1 as construction costs surge
news.para: Ahmedabad homebuyers will face a 5-10% price increase on under-construction properties starting July 1. This decision by Credai Ahmedabad follows a sharp rise in construction material costs, with some items up to 80% costlier due to West Asia conflict-induced supply disruptions. Developers are also seeking a six-month extension on project completion timelines.

news.title: Over 15 property owners booked for tenant verification lapses in Samba
news.para: Jammu and Kashmir Police have filed 19 FIRs against property owners in Samba district for failing to provide tenant details. This action stems from a special verification drive aimed at enhancing security and ensuring compliance with mandatory tenant verification norms. The District Magistrate had ordered the police verification of tenants, but owners did not comply, leading to legal action.

news.title: NCZ restoration puts focus on delayed mapping of Aravali, conservation zones
news.para: In April 2022, National Green Tribunal (NGT) had ordered the Board to complete mapping of NCZ. Experts working for protection of Aravali and NCZ in NCR said the Board should expedite the process to comply with the tribunal’s order. While UP and Rajasthan have completed the delineation of NCZ after carrying out the ground truthing exercise, Haryana and Delhi are still in the process of finalising this.

news.title: Noida: Residents of Mahagun Moderne oppose common area maintenance fee hike
news.para: Residents of Mahagun Moderne Society protested a hike in maintenance charges. They also raised concerns about blocked fire exits and alleged undemocratic AOA elections. Residents demand accountability and rollback of charges. The AOA president stated the hike complies with government orders and dismissed safety violation claims.

news.title: Mumbai records 70 redevelopment agreements in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: According to Knight Frank India, Mumbai’s redevelopment pipeline could unlock nearly 59,000 homes worth about ₹1.5 lakh crore by 2031. Society redevelopment projects are expected to generate about ₹9,115 crore in stamp duty revenues over their project lifecycle.

news.title: Shriram Properties to jointly develop 9.1 acres land in Bengaluru
news.para: Shriram Properties has entered into a joint development agreement for a 9.1-acre land parcel in Doddagubbi, North-East Bengaluru. This project boasts a development potential of 6.7 lakh sq ft and an estimated gross development value exceeding ₹600 crore. The company anticipates launching the project within the current financial year, citing the region's strong residential growth prospects.
